summaries.content: This lesson introduces the story 'Dost Ki Poshak' (दोस्त की पोशाक) from the Rimjhim textbook. The story teaches an important lesson about not judging people by their clothes or outward appearance. A man is invited to a feast at a friend's house. When he arrives wearing simple, old clothes, the people at the gathering do not treat him with respect. They ignore him and give him a poor seat. The lesson sets up the central conflict: should people be judged by what they wear?
keyPoints: The story is about a friend who visits a feast wearing simple clothes
The people at the feast disrespect him because of his appearance
The story raises the question: should we judge people by their clothes?

summaries.content: This lesson continues the story. The same friend returns to the feast, this time wearing expensive, fine clothes. Now everyone treats him with great honour - they offer him the best seat, the finest food, and shower him with attention. The friend realises that the respect was for his clothes, not for him as a person. To teach everyone a lesson, he starts putting food on his clothes instead of eating it. When people are shocked and ask why, he says: 'You are honouring my clothes, not me - so let my clothes enjoy the food!' This clever act exposes the shallow behaviour of the people.
keyPoints: The friend returns wearing expensive clothes and is treated with great respect
He realises the respect is for his clothes, not for him as a person
He puts food on his clothes to teach everyone a lesson
His message: 'You respect my clothes, not me - let my clothes eat!'

summaries.content: This lesson focuses on the vocabulary and language skills from the chapter. Students learn important words like पोशाक (outfit), सम्मान (respect), अपमान (disrespect), दिखावा (show-off), गुण (qualities), and सादगी (simplicity). The lesson teaches opposite words (विलोम शब्द) such as सम्मान-अपमान and सादा-भड़कीला. Students also learn how to express opinions in Hindi using phrases like 'मेरे विचार में...' (In my opinion...) and 'मुझे लगता है...' (I think that...).
keyPoints: New vocabulary: पोशाक, सम्मान, अपमान, दिखावा, गुण, सादगी
Opposite words (विलोम शब्द): सम्मान ↔ अपमान, सादा ↔ भड़कीला
Expressing opinions: मेरे विचार में..., मुझे लगता है...
Understanding the difference between outer appearance and inner qualities

summaries.content: This lesson covers comprehension exercises and values discussion based on the chapter. Students answer questions about the story, discuss what true friendship and respect mean, and share examples from their own life when appearance did not matter. The lesson connects to the Indian value of 'अतिथि देवो भव' (the guest is like God), teaching that every guest should be respected equally. Students also discuss how they would feel if they were treated differently because of their clothes, building empathy and understanding.
keyPoints: Comprehension questions test understanding of the story and its moral
Discussion on true friendship: valuing people for who they are
Connection to 'अतिथि देवो भव' - every guest deserves equal respect
Empathy building: how would you feel if judged by your clothes?
